messages.content: Hey everyone, can we hop on a quick sync about the app launch timeline? I feel like we're all kind of working in silos right now.
Yeah absolutely, I've been meaning to bring this up too. I have a few open questions on the design side that are blocking me from moving forward.
Same here on the backend. There are a couple of API endpoints I haven't scoped yet because I'm waiting on the final feature list.
Before we get into it — does anyone else think it's freezing in this office today? I can barely type, my fingers are numb.
Ha, yes! I've had my jacket on since 9am. Anyway, let's try to focus — I want to nail down a launch date first and work backwards from there.
That makes sense. What dates are we actually considering? I know we talked loosely about mid-March but nothing was ever confirmed.
I was thinking March 15th. It gives us enough runway after the holidays and it's before Q2 which is when marketing wants to push the campaign.
March 15th works for me from a design standpoint, as long as I know what I'm designing by early February. I can't absorb a bunch of last-minute feature adds.
Understood. Backend-wise I think it's tight but doable. What does QA think? You guys are the ones who usually get squeezed at the end.
Honestly, if we're launching March 15th, I need at minimum ten days of testing. Like a hard ten days, not a 'we'll see how it goes' ten days.
Ten days is fair. So that would put the testing window at March 1st through the 10th, and then we have a few days for final fixes and deployment prep.
That actually works. QA testing window March 1 through 10 — I'll block that off officially and make sure the team is heads-down on this and nothing else is scheduled.
Perfect. So March 15th is our launch date, everyone aligned on that?
Yep, I'm good with March 15th.
Same, March 15th works for me.
Locked in. March 15th it is. I'll update the project board and send a calendar invite to the broader team after this call.
Great. Now working backwards — if QA starts March 1st, that means we need a code freeze by what, February 28th?
Yeah February 28th for code freeze makes sense. That gives QA the weekend to set up test environments if they need to.
I can commit to having all the APIs done by February 28th. It's going to be a push but I'll make it work. I'll flag early if anything is slipping.
Appreciate that. And I'll hold you to the early flag — nothing worse than finding out on the 27th that something's not ready.
Noted, fair. Hey before I forget — did anyone try that new espresso machine in the kitchen? Whoever set it up has it dialed in perfectly.
Oh that was me actually, I brought my own beans from the place on Fifth. The office stuff was undrinkable, I couldn't take it anymore.
Honestly a hero move. Okay back to the plan — design, what do you need from me to get moving on the final mockups?
I need the confirmed feature set in writing, and I need a decision on whether we're doing the onboarding flow v1 or the simplified version. Those two things are blocking me.
Feature set I can send you today. On the onboarding flow, I'm going to say let's go with the simplified version for launch and we can iterate after. Less risk.

Okay simplified onboarding it is. If you send me the feature list today, I can commit to delivering the final mockups by next Monday. That gives the dev team time to start building before end of month.
Perfect, that's exactly what I needed to hear. I'll get you that doc by end of day.
sounds good
Backend, any dependencies you need from design before you can start on the core stuff?

Mostly just the data model assumptions baked into the screens. Like if there's a profile page with certain fields, I need to make sure the schema matches. I can start on auth and the core infra while I wait though.
Smart, start on auth now and we'll sync once the mockups land. QA, are you going to need a staging environment set up ahead of March 1st?
Yes, I'll need staging ready by February 25th at the latest. I want a few days to write test plans against the actual environment, not just the designs.
February 25th for staging — backend, is that on your radar?
Yeah I can have staging up by the 25th, that's not a problem. The environment setup is pretty straightforward once the core services are in place.
ok great
Should we also talk about the external beta? I know marketing mentioned wanting a small beta group before the full launch but I don't know if that's still on the table.

Good call. I talked to marketing last week and they scaled that back. We're going to do a soft launch to a small internal group on March 13th, just to catch anything obvious before the public launch on the 15th.
Oh that's way more manageable than a full beta. Two days of internal testing, that works.
yeah it keeps things simple
What's everyone doing this weekend by the way? I feel like I haven't actually talked to any of you like a human being in weeks.
I'm going hiking if the weather cooperates. Been staring at screens too much. What about you?
Probably just catching up on sleep honestly. My toddler has been waking up at 5am every day and I'm running on fumes.
Oh man, respect. Okay we should probably wrap up and let you get back to it. Let me just recap what we decided.
Please do, I want to make sure I didn't miss anything.

Launch date is March 15th. QA testing window is March 1st through 10th. Backend has all APIs done by February 28th. Designer delivers final mockups by next Monday. Staging ready by February 25th. And I'm sending the feature list to design today.
That's a solid summary. One thing I'd add — can we schedule a mid-point check-in around February 14th? Just a short one to make sure nothing has gone sideways before we hit the code freeze.
Yes, good idea. February 14th check-in, I'll send an invite. Valentine's Day standup, how romantic.
Ha, can we at least have donuts or something
I'll bring donuts, deal. Okay anything else before we close out?
I think we're good. This was actually really productive, I feel way less anxious about the timeline now that it's all written down.
Agreed, same. It's amazing what one focused hour can do versus a week of Slack threads going nowhere.
Don't even get me started on the Slack threads. I have 47 unread messages in the app-launch channel and I haven't opened it in two days.
lol same honestly
Alright I'm going to go make another coffee — the good kind — and start pulling together the auth service scaffolding. Thanks everyone, this was needed.
Thanks all. I'll have that feature doc in your inbox by 5pm. Let's crush this.
Let's do it. And seriously someone please talk to facilities about the temperature in here, it is not okay.
I submitted a ticket last Tuesday, they said they're 'looking into it.' So probably never.
Classic. Alright, talk soon everyone. Good meeting.
Good meeting. See you all Thursday.
